reasoning:
The FRED series RPONTSYD tracks the Federal Reserve's daily repurchase agreement operations (adding reserves via the Standing Repo Facility/OMO). 

1. Baseline calculation: The question evaluates against the value on 2025-03-30 (a Sunday). Because FRED daily OMO series do not print on weekends, the standard evaluation maps to the preceding business day, Friday 2025-03-28. Our FRED API pull confirms the value on 03-28 was 0.001. Therefore, to resolve YES, the value on each resolution date must be strictly greater than 0.001 (i.e., 0.002 or higher).
2. Base rate & Short-term (Apr 6, Apr 29): Currently, daily values are predominantly 0.0, with test operations often registering exactly 0.001. Only about 15-20% of days see usage >0.001. Since April 6 is a Sunday (proxy April 4), the likelihood of usage exceeding the 0.001 baseline on a random early-month day is low. I estimate 16% and 24% respectively.
3. Medium-to-Long-term (Jun 28, Sep 26, Mar 30 2026): Quantitative Tightening (QT) is currently draining roughly $40B in reserves per month. Over the next 6-12 months, reserves are expected to approach the minimum 'ample' threshold. As this happens, liquidity friction in the banking system will increase, meaning SRF/Repo operations will transition from occasional $1M 'test' usages (0.001) to routine liquidity injections (values reaching 0.1 or much larger). 
4. Furthermore, Sept 26 and Mar 30 coincide closely with quarter-ends, dramatically increasing the structural need for overnight liquidity. Therefore, probabilities rise steadily as the fundamental liquidity architecture of the financial system shifts from abundant to ample, hitting 54% for the 1-year horizon.
